class_0_no_dr.description: This class is explicitly characterized by the presence of normal morphological landmarks and the absolute absence of any pathological lesions.

class_1_mild_npdr.description: The earliest stage of pathology, strictly bounded by the presence of microaneurysms only, with no other advanced lesions.

class_2_moderate_npdr.description: Characterized by the appearance of true intraretinal hemorrhages and exudative changes, but falling short of the "4-2-1" rule for severe disease.

notes.foveal_avascular_zone: Can widen due to early capillary non-perfusion
notes.circinate_hard_exudate: Forming a localized ring around a leaking microaneurysm

class_3_severe_npdr.description: Defined strictly by the ETDRS 4-2-1 rule: ≥20 intraretinal hemorrhages in 4 quadrants, definite venous beading in ≥2 quadrants, or prominent IRMA in ≥1 quadrant.

notes.blot_hemorrhage: Dense, dark hemorrhages typically counting toward the 4-quadrant rule
notes.four_quadrant_hemorrhage: Meeting the critical multi-quadrant ETDRS criteria threshold
notes.venous_omega_loop: Advanced mechanical deformation of the venous wall

class_4_proliferative_dr.description: The final stage, defined by extraretinal fibrovascular proliferation or gross vitreoretinal consequences.

notes.peripapillary_neovascular_net: Fragile new vessels spreading from the immediate margin of the disc
